commit.message:
fix: setup_rocm.sh handles pre-existing CUDA repos on vast.ai

Search common paths for nvcc (not just PATH), clear conflicting
keyring files before installing cuda-keyring. Fixes "Conflicting
values set for option Signed-By" on vast.ai H100 containers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Close remaining feature gaps from issue #50 (#62)

- Source video restoration: embed="source" now replaces embedded videos
  with their sourceVideo references before writing, matching Python's
  write_videos(restore_source=True) behavior
- Format 1.2 tracking_score: explicit formatId < 1.2 guard zeroes
  tracking_score for old formats; also handles NaN values in all three
  readers (eager, streaming, lazy)
- Lazy toNumpy() fast path: LazyDataStore.toNumpy() builds 4D array
  directly from raw column data without materializing frames; Labels.numpy()
  delegates to it when in lazy mode

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add get_full_name to CustomUser for bugsnag compatibility

Bugsnag middleware calls request.user.get_full_name() which doesn't
exist on our email-only CustomUser model. Returns email address.

commit.message:
CLIF **v3.0.0** Data Dictionary, mCIDE and Change Log updates

### New Tables
- New **input** concept table — captures patient fluid intake events with hierarchical categorization (input_category → input_group), replacing the intake side of the former `intake_output` table. [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/Common-Longitudinal-ICU-data-Format/CLIF/issues/179)

### Removed Tables
- Removed **intake_output** concept table — replaced by separate `input` and `output` tables

commit.message:
STRUCTURES.md: fixes, justifications, and map.js coverage

Structural fixes:
- Phase 2 field normalization: add batching rule (one struct type per
  commit) and split Deleted row by struct type for mechanical verifiability
- game.js bootstrap section: rename from 'Phase 2 parallel' to clarify
  it is a prerequisite for the rename sweep, not tied to Phase 2 timing;
  fix code comments ('replaces' -> 'target:') and note game.js imports
  map.js for GameMap; note why objects.js/monsters.js not needed at init
- Merge duplicate 'Between Phase 3 and Phase 4' headings; youmonst notes
  folded into the rename sweep section

Justifications added:
- 'Fix: runtime wiring discipline' section: explain why initAll is not
  needed (function-only cycles + ESM live bindings + declarative top-level)
- Phase 2 ordering: explain prerequisite for autotranslator correctness
- game.u sub-object: cross-reference Hero state nesting rationale

map.js coverage (omission fixed):
- Add game.level / GameMap section in Struct Types: documents JS
  consolidation of svl.level + svr.rooms + svd.doors + level lists;
  full location tile (struct rm) field table; note map.js is a near-leaf
  that stays through all phases
- Update game.rooms[] section to note JS location (game.level.rooms[])
- Final step exit gate: explicitly list map.js as kept

commit.message:
Fix label stock type: STOCK_4X6 -> PAPER_4X6 for PNG output

FedEx API rejects STOCK_4X6 with PNG imageType. PAPER_4X6 is the
correct stock type for 4x6 PNG labels.

- Create src/lib/qbo/client.ts: qboRequest() and qboQuery() for authenticated QBO API calls
- Auto token refresh via getValidAccessToken() (5-min expiry buffer)
- Retry logic: 401 (re-auth), 429 (rate limit with Retry-After), 5xx (server errors)
- QBOApiError class with statusCode, intuitTid, and retryable flag
- Sandbox/production base URL switching via QBO_ENVIRONMENT
- Barrel export in src/lib/qbo/index.ts

- Polling progressivo: ±150 → ±300 → sem limite de ELO
- PvPMatchPage: batalha 5 rounds com countdown, flash feedback, animações
- TournamentPage: brackets eliminatórias com visualização
- TournamentManagerPage: criação/gestão de campeonatos (professor)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 405 on save/share/checkout: use direct Function App URLs

SWA route rewrites don't forward POST requests. Updated all StoryForge
JS modules to call the Azure Function App directly for POST endpoints
(save, gallery publish, checkout). Also improved AI prompts to enforce
item consistency between narrative and stateChanges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.4.0: City economy, build agent, fully convolutional network, ranged combat

Major features:
- City economy: tile-based food/production, population growth/starvation,
  priority-based tile assignment (food desc, prod desc)
- Build agent: separate DQN choosing city production (7 options: 5 unit
  types + settler + granary), build state encoded as 9 extra channels
- Fully convolutional network: map-size independent architecture using
  1x1 convolutions instead of FC layers. Same weights work on any map.
- Ranged combat: Archers/Catapults can attack at range 2 without
  counterattack damage. Environment no longer blocks non-adjacent attacks
  for ranged units.
- Settler founding: select settler + same tile = found city
- EnhancedStateEncoder (25ch) wired up with --encoder flag
- SharedBackboneNetwork added

Bug fixes:
- Attack always consumes all movement points (was only on kill)
- Fortification/healing per-turn via has_acted flag (was per-step)
- Removed double end_turn() in _check_game_end()

Documentation:
- documents/state_spaces.md: complete state space reference
- documents/implementation_plan.md: restructured priorities (game
  complexity before model refinement)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix print: override CSS variables for light theme, remove dark backgrounds

- Override all CSS custom properties (--bg, --card, etc.) in print to light values
- Add universal background reset (* { background-color: transparent })
- Explicit white background on #app, .content, #section-auswertung

commit.message:
fix(c5): replace file-type ESM dynamic import with inline magic bytes detection

Jest's module resolver cannot find the ESM-only 'file-type' package when
imported via dynamic import() in a ts-jest (CommonJS) test environment,
causing all uploads to fall into the catch block and return 400.

Replace with a self-contained detectImageMagicBytes() function that reads
the first 12 bytes from disk using fs.readSync — no external deps, works
in CJS, ESM, and Jest contexts. Detects JPEG (FF D8 FF), PNG (8-byte sig),
and WebP (RIFF....WEBP) signatures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(whatsapp): remove false-positive is_connected after build_and_run_bot

build_and_run_bot unconditionally stored the client reference before
bot.run(), making is_connected() return true even when the bot hadn't
connected yet. This short-circuited the QR pairing flow with "already
connected" on dormant on-demand start. The Connected event handler
already stores the client when the connection is actually established.

commit.message:
opus-2026-03-05-S2: THM-013 arc-flip ΔI formula, major Q-009 progress

Key discovery: explicit formula for how I(Ω(T),2) changes under arc flips:
  ΔI = -2·Σ_x s_x·H(B_x) + 2·(D5-C5)
Verified 2216/2216 at n=6. Reduces OCF (=Claim A) to adjacency identity.

Also proved: at n=5, Ω(T) is always complete, giving simple proof of OCF.

New: THM-013, T028, T029, 10 computation scripts, kind-pasteur message.

fix: make wildcard_imports deny effective for production code (#222)

Remove crate-level #![allow(wildcard_imports)] from lib.rs and main.rs
that was overriding the Cargo.toml deny. Move wildcard_imports to the
cfg_attr(test, allow(...)) block so test modules using `use super::*;`
still compile. Add targeted #[allow(clippy::wildcard_imports)] to the
one production wildcard import: `use super::proto::*;` in trait_impl.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(S60): sprint 60 scorecard + codebase map (#119)

* feat(S60): compaction-proof review gates

review-tier guard now writes review-state.json on plan detection so
workflow-gate mechanically blocks ExitPlanMode until reviews complete.
Compaction guard captures review state and sprint phase in handoff data.
workflow-gate deny message includes actionable recovery steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(guards): add worktree-merge guard to block --delete-branch in worktrees

`gh pr merge --delete-branch` succeeds at merging but exits 1 when local
branch cleanup fails (worktree holds main). The agent sees the error,
retries, gets "already merged". This guard detects the pattern and blocks
with the correct command, preventing the false-failure loop in any repo
using SLOPE hooks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(guards): add worktree-merge guard unit tests

6 tests: non-merge passthrough, no --delete-branch passthrough,
main working tree passthrough, worktree block, short -d flag,
and git failure fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(S60): add sprint 60 scorecard and update codebase map

Par sprint — 5 tickets, 0 hazards. Codebase map updated to reflect
21 guards (worktree-merge added).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix sacrifice detection with SEE, extend PV forcing for en-prise pieces, add game replay

- Sacrifice label now uses SEE to verify net material loss — defended pieces
  (e.g. Ng4 where Nf6 recaptures) no longer falsely labeled as sacrifices
- PV analysis extends through opponent captures on en-prise pieces (≥ minor),
  fixing puzzles that were truncated to 1 move (e.g. puzzle #23131: 1→2 moves)
- Add tactical climax detection to PV analysis (stop extending after decisive
  material gain)
- Add game move replay with back/forward navigation in puzzle solver
- Expose game PGN and puzzle ply in puzzle detail endpoint
- Track puzzle move history for analysis mode navigation
